Hey, it's Phil again. In this video, I'm going to step you through the automation for MVL 2.0 for triggered automation 1A. If you refer to your workbook, you'll see that there's DX1A early access with expiring code. You've already been walked through this entire strategy. I'm going to refer to an example that was from one of my launches. If we go to example 3 here, this is the example we're going to work off of. You can see where we are allowing our prospects, our pre-customers to click a link to get access to a special pre-sale. When you click that link or when they click that link, it will likely land them on the sales page unexpectedly, and they will be excited to have early access. But what you'll want to do is then trigger your email 1A. Well, how do we do that? You're going to set a link trigger action on your pre-sale links within that pre-launch email, and I'll step you through that in a separate video. But what's going to happen is when that link trigger is set, you'll have a trigger tag. The format here we have is trigger colon 1A, early access with expiring code. The name is relatively arbitrary. I've named it what it is so that it's very easy to follow with our handbook and our templates. When that tag is added, this automation will become active for the customer, the pre-customer who clicked the link. What I'm then going to do is wait for 15 minutes. This is somewhat optional, but the idea here is that if you are, when someone is clicking the link, if you're sending them to the sales page, well, they're going to be in an immediate process of exploration and diving into the sales page. We want to give them a little bit of time to explore so that they might just go ahead and make the purchase. The next thing we're doing here is we are checking to see what day of the week it is. You'll want this to align to the strategy of when your launch is happening. In our hypothetical launch, if you've watched my previous video, we said the first email for pre-launch was happening on Monday. That first coupon code was also valid only on Monday. What we want to do is say, hey, is it actually Monday? Because if it's not, we're going to end the automation. There's more sophisticated stuff we could do here, but I found that this is the minimal effective dose in terms of the automation. Of course, you could also omit that step if you just wanted to let someone who clicked the link late get the code. That's really all up to you in terms of how you want to be integrity to your own launch. Essentially, after we filter for the day, then we are going to do, just like we do in the main sale sequence, we're going to look and see, hey, do they have the mute tag or have they already purchased? Because if they have, we're just going to go ahead and end this automation because we don't need to send them an additional email about this. But if they haven't muted, they haven't purchased, we're going to go ahead and send that Dayax 1.8 early access with expiring code email. And that's it. Once we've sent that email, that ends or completes this automation. And so with any other automation, once you have this set the way you want and you've written your copy and added it in, you'll simply activate by choosing active and you'll be ready to go.
